evals.prompt: Plan a refactor for a mobile screen. Do not edit. Reduce parent branching, preserve behavior, follow project conventions, and name the target file plus nearby pattern files you would read.
evals.expected_output: A scoped implementation plan grounded in target and nearby files, with no file edits.
expectations: The output states that no files were edited.
The output names the target file and at least two nearby pattern files to read.
The output includes a Component Boundary Audit.
The output distinguishes absence modeled with Maybe from renderable variants modeled with discriminated unions.
The output includes the owning package typecheck and lint plan.

evals.prompt: Refactor a mobile component so the child owns optional UI and presentation variants. Keep behavior unchanged and follow the code-pattern skill.
evals.expected_output: A narrow component refactor that moves presentation variant logic into the owning child without changing shared contracts.
expectations: The parent no longer has match or switch branches that return the same child component with different props.
Absence is represented with Maybe when the type is local and render-only.
The child owns variant rendering and presentation copy.
Touched components use named prop types.
Conditional className values use cn() where the local stack supports it.
The output introduces no new dependency.

evals.prompt: Replace a mobile sign-in submit flow with the package form/API pattern. Use the local helpers and avoid frontend-only imports.
evals.expected_output: A mobile submit flow using the local form abstraction, typed API call, Future handling, and loading/error state.
expectations: The implementation uses useForm with local field config classes.
The implementation renders controlled fields with FormInput when exported.
The submit path calls call(api.signIn, ...).
The Future result is handled with .fork(onError, onSuccess).
Submit state uses RemoteData or the nearest local async state pattern.
Inputs are disabled while submitting.
The implementation does not use raw fetch.
The implementation does not import @fe/* from mobile code.

evals.prompt: Fix a frontend create/edit flow so success refetch waits for projected read models. Preserve request construction through the existing API helper.
evals.expected_output: A frontend write success path that schedules read-model-dependent work through the local projection-delay hook.
expectations: The implementation imports useProjectionDelay from the frontend hook path.
The success path runs refetch, close, or navigation inside schedule.
Requests are constructed with call(api.someEndpoint, body).
Submit or save state uses RemoteData or the nearest local async state pattern.
The implementation does not import mobile aliases.
The implementation does not add an ad hoc timeout helper.

evals.prompt: Refactor a DTO parsing and state model to match the TypeScript conventions. Preserve local framework boundaries.
evals.expected_output: A type-safe domain refactor that uses local ID, union, decoder/schema, and error-handling patterns without over-normalizing framework code.
expectations: The implementation uses Id<T> or an existing local ID type for entity identifiers.
The state model uses a discriminated union instead of a bag of optional flags.
Recoverable absence uses Maybe where the boundary does not own null or undefined.
Incoming JSON is validated with a decoder or schema before typed use.
Recoverable parsing failures return Result or the local equivalent.
The implementation avoids app-level any.
The implementation does not replace framework-required Promise contracts with Future.
